verse.arabic_text: قُلْ إِنَّ صَلَاتِى وَنُسُكِى وَمَحْيَاىَ وَمَمَاتِى لِلَّهِ رَبِّ ٱلْعَـٰلَمِينَ
verse.translation: Say, 'Indeed, my prayer, my rites of sacrifice, my living and my dying are for Allah, Lord of the worlds.'

theme_question.correct: The complete devotion of one's entire life to Allah.
options: The complete devotion of one's entire life to Allah.
The specific rituals of the annual pilgrimage (Hajj).
The importance of remembering death to stay humble.
The obligation of the five daily prayers.

educational_notes.verse_context: This verse is a powerful declaration of faith, commanded by Allah for the Prophet Muhammad (ﷺ) and all believers to proclaim. It comes towards the end of Surah Al-An'am, summarizing the core message of the surah after detailed arguments against polytheism and discussions on Allah's absolute sovereignty. It serves as a personal and public pledge of one's entire existence to the Creator.
educational_notes.theme_explanation: The central theme is Tawhid al-Uluhiyyah (Oneness of Worship). The verse beautifully encapsulates the concept that a believer's life, in its entirety—from acts of worship like prayer (salati) and sacrifice (nusuki) to the very processes of living (mahyaya) and dying (mamati)—is solely for Allah. It rejects any division of loyalty and affirms that every moment and action should be an act of submission to the Lord of all worlds.
educational_notes.surah_overview: Surah Al-An'am ('The Cattle') is the 6th chapter of the Quran and is unique as it was revealed entirely in one night in Makkah. Its primary focus is on establishing the foundations of faith (Aqidah), particularly the oneness of Allah (Tawhid). It refutes the polytheistic beliefs of the Quraysh by presenting logical arguments and drawing attention to Allah's signs in creation. The surah also discusses prophethood, revelation, and the Day of Judgment, without legislating many practical laws, which is characteristic of Makkan surahs.
